news.headline: French Far Right Falls Short of Statement Win in Yardstick Local Races
news.summary: France’s far right hoped for major gains in Sunday’s municipal elections, a key bellwether moment before a presidential election next year. Its results were mixed.

news.headline: Starmer calls emergency meeting on Iran war’s economic impact on UK
news.summary:
British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is set to chair an emergency meeting ⁠on the economic fallout from the ⁠war in Iran on Monday, with Finance Minister Rachel ⁠Reeves and Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey, the government said.
Investors are bracing for another stormy week in financial markets

news.headline: Kashmiris donate gold and cash to support Iran
news.summary: Residents in Indian-administered Kashmir are donating their gold possessions and cash to support Iranians.

news.headline: Hong Kong’s first MRI-guided ultrasound offers relief for Parkinson’s tremors
news.summary:
Tuen Mun Hospital has, for the first time in Hong Kong, used a non-invasive, MRI-guided ultrasound procedure to treat a Parkinson’s disease patient suffering from uncontrollable tremors, offering an alternative option for those affected by the condition.

news.headline: Potential rate cut pause, geopolitical tensions may cloud Hong Kong property recovery
news.summary:
Hongkonger Katie Chan was hoping to buy a flat to live in, and although the 37-year-old accountant was in no rush, the potential longer pause in interest rate cuts could delay her decision.
For Chan, the ideal mortgage would be if the one-month Hong Kong interbank offered rate (Hibor) fell below 1.9

news.headline: France's Socialists hold onto power in major cities in election boost for mainstream
news.summary: Paris and Marseille are held by the Socialists in local elections which saw the nationalist right win in Nice and certain towns.

news.headline: West Bank settler violence surges as Israeli strikes in Gaza kill 4 Palestinians
news.summary: At least 10 Palestinians were injured on Sunday in settler attacks near Nablus in the occupied West Bank, Palestinian medics said. news.headline: Trump says ICE agents will assist airport security as DHS shutdown continues
news.summary: Travellers have faced hours-long waits at airports around the country as a partial government shutdown has left TSA agents without pay.

news.headline: Socialist Emmanuel Gregoire wins Paris mayoral race
news.summary: Gregoire headed a list uniting the traditional left, the Greens and the Communists to victory in French capital.

news.headline: Iran threatens to cripple Gulf water and energy systems after Trump ultimatum
news.summary: Iran said on Sunday it would target Gulf energy and water infrastructure if U.S. President Donald Trump follows through on a threat to strike its power grid within 48 hours, escalating the conflict. news.headline: Israeli settlers target Palestinian villages in occupied West Bank, attacking people and properties
news.summary: The violence began after 18-year-old settler Yehuda Sherman was killed after reportedly being hit by a vehicle driven by a Palestinian while on his quad bike.

news.headline: Vinicius strikes twice as Real Madrid edge Atletico 3-2 in pulsating derby
news.summary: Real stay within four points of league leaders Barcelona with hard-fought win in Madrid derby.

news.headline: Election in Slovenia Is a Near Tie
news.summary: The center-left and the right-wing populists got about equal support. The next government will be led by the one who can build a coalition with smaller parties.

news.headline: Who’s left running Iran?
news.summary: Much of the top leadership has been eliminated, but the country is still fighting.

news.headline: France’s municipal elections : Emmanuel Grégoire elected Paris mayor
news.summary: “Paris is not, and will never be, a far-right city,” insisted socialist Emmanuel Grégoire on Sunday March 22, newly elected Mayor of Paris, according to early results.#EmmanuelGrégoire #ParisMayor
